# GenXAI — full reference for LLMs

GenXAI is an open-source agentic AI framework for Python, licensed entirely under MIT.
There is no commercial edition: the core framework and all production-grade runtime
features (connectors, triggers, observability, security, CLI) are open source in one
repository: https://github.com/genexsus-ai/genxai-framework

Website: https://genxai-framework.com
Install: pip install genxai-framework
Optional extras: llm, tools, api, storage, observability, connectors, all
  (e.g. pip install "genxai-framework[llm,tools,api]")

## Core capabilities

### Graph-based orchestration
Workflows are directed graphs, not linear chains: conditional edges and dynamic
routing, parallel + sequential execution with real join semantics, cycles/loops,
nested subgraphs, checkpoints and resume. Multi-agent flow patterns can be embedded
as single agent-team (FLOW) nodes inside a graph.

### Flows: 14 coordination patterns
Parallel, Router, Conditional, Loop, Round-robin, Selector, Critic review,
Ensemble voting, Map-reduce, Coordinator-worker, Delegator, Subworkflow,
P2P, Auction.

### Agents
Configurable roles, goals, models, tools, and memory. Agent factory + registry,
guardrails and constraints, multi-provider LLM routing, agent presets and library,
AssistantAgent and UserProxyAgent types.

### Memory system
Layered: short-term, long-term, episodic, semantic, procedural, working.
Backend plugins: Redis, SQLite, Neo4j. Consolidation, retrieval,
importance-based retention, shared memory bus for multi-agent runs, telemetry
via MemorySystem.get_stats().

### Tools
45 built-in tools across web, data, file, database, computation, and communication
categories. Custom tool creation, dynamic tool creation, tool registry with
templates, secure execution. MCP (Model Context Protocol) support as both client
and server — agents can call tools from any registered MCP server.

### LLM providers
OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Cohere, Ollama. Multi-provider routing lets different
agents in one workflow use different models. Opt-in LLM ranking for tool selection.

### Connectors (all MIT, included)
Slack, GitHub, Jira, Notion, Google Workspace, HubSpot, WhatsApp, Kafka, SQS,
Postgres (incl. CDC), S3, SMTP email, generic webhooks.

### Triggers
Webhook, schedule (cron), queue, and file-watcher triggers start workflows from
external events.

### Production runtime
Observability: logging, metrics (Prometheus), tracing. Security: RBAC, policy
engine, audit logging, rate limits. CLI commands: tool, workflow, connector,
metrics, approval, audit. REST API server, Dockerfile, docker-compose, and
Kubernetes manifests included.

### No-Code Workflow Studio
An n8n-style drag-and-drop visual builder (applications/workflow_studio in the
repository, also its own repo): compose agent/tool pipelines on a canvas, run
them with live per-node status, automate with webhooks and schedules.
Hosted demo: https://app.genxflowstudio.com — marketing site: https://genxflowstudio.com

## Quick start

    pip install genxai-framework

    import os
    from genxai import Agent, AgentConfig, AgentRegistry, Graph

    os.environ["OPENAI_API_KEY"] = "sk-..."

    support = Agent(
        id="support",
        config=AgentConfig(role="Support Agent", goal="Resolve customer issues",
                           llm_model="claude-3-opus", enable_memory=True),
    )
    AgentRegistry.register(support)

    graph = Graph()
    # add nodes + edges, then:
    result = await graph.run(input_data="My app crashed")
